key_insight: The paper presents an open-weight multilingual pipeline that turns news text into signed, temporal political-elite knowledge graphs by jointly extracting entities and directed relations across languages.
engineering_relevance: It offers a scalable, reproducible alternative to manual coding and proprietary LLM APIs for building structured relational datasets from large unstructured corpora.
method: Span-based NER identifies person and organization mentions in multilingual news.
A three-stage linking cascade resolves mentions to language-independent Wikidata IDs.
An ontology-constrained mixture-of-experts model with guided decoding extracts directed, signed relations.
evidence_signal: Against a 3,491-relation gold standard, the pipeline achieved 68.2% strict and 93.7% lenient textual correctness, with case studies in Austria and Poland matching known political developments.
watch_item: Entity resolution and relation extraction remain sensitive to cross-lingual ambiguity and ontology coverage, so performance may drop in noisier corpora or underrepresented languages.
paper_title: Mapping Political-Elite Networks in Europe with a Multilingual Joint Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
